skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem:
−x − vy = −341

2x − vy = 19

In the given system of equations, v is a constant. In the xy-plane, the graphs of these equations intersect at the point (p, 17), where p is a constant. What is the value of v?
rationale: The correct answer is 13. Since the point (p, 17) lies on both lines, substitute it into both equations. From the first equation, −p − 17v = −341, so p = 341 − 17v. From the second equation, 2p − 17v = 19. Substitute p = 341 − 17v into this: 2(341 − 17v) − 17v = 19, or 682 − 34v − 17v = 19. Combining like terms gives 682 − 51v = 19, so 51v = 663, and v = 13.
